page: Microsoft Azure L8s_v2 is a storage-focused virtual machine from the LSv2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 7551 processor, operating at a base clock speed of 3.2 GHz. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. For local storage, the instance includes 2002 GB of Premium Storage-capable capacity. It does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark evaluations indicate that the L8s_v2 delivers modest processing and memory performance. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance scored 698.49, placing it in the bottom 10%, while the multi-core stress-ng score of 2347.46 sits in the bottom 25%. Memory bandwidth tests show a read-only small block cached speed of 304.23 GB/sec, but uncached large block read and write speeds drop to 19.02 GB/sec and 12.95 GB/sec, respectively, both falling into the bottom 10%. Passmark CPU and memory scores are average, with a CPU score of 6937.84 and a memory score of 2184.96.

The primary tradeoff of the L8s_v2 is its lower computational and memory throughput relative to its high local storage capacity and memory footprint. While CPU-bound tasks like software compilation and text processing perform weakly, the instance maintains average performance in cryptographic tasks and small-model LLM text generation. Given the high local storage density and memory capacity, this instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for storage-intensive workloads that require large local scratch space but do not demand high CPU performance, such as local database caching, NoSQL databases, and large-scale data processing.
description: Microsoft Azure L8s_v2 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the LSv2 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 7551 processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 4 physical cores running at 3.2 GHz. It features 64.0 GB of RAM and 2002 GB of Premium Storage-capable local storage, with no integrated GPU. Performance benchmarks indicate modest CPU and memory throughput, with stress-ng single-core and uncached memory bandwidth falling into the bottom 10%, while Passmark CPU and memory scores remain average. The instance is qualitatively cost-effective for storage-heavy workloads that require substantial local capacity and memory but have moderate CPU demands. It is best suited for databases, local caching, and data processing tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure L8s_v2 is an LSv2-family virtual machine featuring 8 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB of RAM, and 2002 GB of local storage, designed for storage-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure L8s_v2 features 8 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 2002 GB local storage, optimized for storage-heavy database workloads.
tagline: An x86 server combining 64 GB of memory with 2 TB of local storage for storage-intensive and database workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7551 processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 4 physical cores.
Equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM and 2002 GB of local Premium Storage.
Delivers average performance in cryptography and small-model LLM text generation.
Shows lower performance in CPU-bound tasks and uncached memory bandwidth.
Suited for local databases, caching layers, and storage-heavy data processing.
